deep_dive.title: Tuning-Free Latent Diffusion Models for Ultrahigh-Resolution Image Editing
deep_dive.abstract: Recent diffusion-based generative models have shown impressive performance in image generation and editing. However, due to memory limitations and the high cost of collecting high-resolution training images, existing methods are typically restricted to inputs with linear resolutions below 1K. In contrast, photos captured by modern mobile devices often reach linear resolutions up to 8K, revealing a significant gap between current capabilities and real-world demands. Simply upscaling low-resolution edited results often results in visually enlarged but blurry images that lack fine details. This paper introduces UltraDiffEdit, a novel, tuning-free image editing framework that extends off-the-shelf latent diffusion models (LDMs) to ultrahigh resolutions. UltraDiffEdit employs a multi-scale progressive editing strategy, iteratively blending high-resolution edited content with unedited areas in a coarse-to-fine manner. We employ multi-patch encoding to preserve both edited and unedited visual details within the latent space. To mitigate editing artifacts, our global-local consistency denoising technique consistently integrates edited and unedited latent features, ensuring smooth transition at editing boundaries from the latent representation to the final image. We also introduce a patch-based hybrid sampling approach that captures local, intermediate, and global features, ensuring semantic coherence and enhancing fine detail during denoising. We conduct extensive experiments demonstrating UltraDiffEdit's superior editing quality and flexibility: it can handle image resolutions up to 8K using only a single NVIDIA GeForce RTX 3090 GPU. The source code is publicly available at https://github.com/LonglongaaaGo/UltraDiffEdit.
deep_dive.llm_evidence_en: Ultrahigh-resolution image editing with tuning-free latent diffusion
deep_dive.llm_evidence_cn: 利用免调优潜在扩散进行超高分辨率图像编辑
deep_dive.llm_evidence: 利用免调优潜在扩散进行超高分辨率图像编辑
deep_dive.canonical_evidence: 利用免调优潜在扩散进行超高分辨率图像编辑
deep_dive.llm_tldr_en: UltraDiffEdit extends latent diffusion models to ultrahigh-resolution image editing without tuning, overcoming memory limits and producing fine details.
deep_dive.llm_tldr_cn: 扩散模型图像编辑受限于低分辨率，无法满足现代设备8K图像处理需求，直接上采样导致模糊。本文提出UltraDiffEdit，一种免调优的超高分辨率图像编辑框架，将预训练潜扩散模型扩展到超高分辨率，无需额外训练即可在8K图像上实现精细编辑。通过高效的空间自适应推理策略，在保持细节的同时编辑全局，实验显示在超高分辨率编辑任务上显著优于现有方法。
deep_dive.llm_tldr: 扩散模型图像编辑受限于低分辨率，无法满足现代设备8K图像处理需求，直接上采样导致模糊。本文提出UltraDiffEdit，一种免调优的超高分辨率图像编辑框架，将预训练潜扩散模型扩展到超高分辨率，无需额外训练即可在8K图像上实现精细编辑。通过高效的空间自适应推理策略，在保持细节的同时编辑全局，实验显示在超高分辨率编辑任务上显著优于现有方法。

deep_dive.title: FADRA: Frequency-Aware Diffusion with Residual Adaptation for Video Face Restoration
deep_dive.abstract: Video face restoration (VFR) aims to recover high-quality and temporally consistent facial details from severely degraded video sequences; however, existing methods still struggle to balance spatial fidelity and temporal coherence under complex degradations. To address this, we propose FADRA, a frequency-aware diffusion framework with iterative residual adaptation specifically tailored for robust VFR. We first leverage the strong temporal consistency of a pre-trained text-to-video diffusion model and introduce lightweight LoRA adapters together with a Low-Quality (LQ) Pixel-Alignment Feature Fusion module to efficiently adapt the frozen generative prior to the VFR task. To further adapt the frozen diffusion backbone to the downstream VFR task beyond LoRA-based adaptation, we introduce a Repeated Residual Adaptation Head (RRAH) for step-wise residual refinement after the diffusion backbone. To make this refinement explicitly guided by the degraded observation, RRAH further takes the LQ latent together with the current velocity prediction as input, allowing the model to repeatedly revisit LQ cues and predict residual updates at each flow-matching step. This LQ-guided repeated residual adaptation helps recover fine facial details while preserving the inherent temporal priors of the pre-trained model. Furthermore, to ensure the structural integrity of perceptually important details, we introduce a Frequency-Aware Loss that provides explicit supervision across multiple spectral bands, emphasizing visually sensitive frequency components that are crucial for perceptual quality and prone to temporal jittering. Extensive experiments demonstrate that FADRA recovers better facial structures and produces more temporally consistent videos than state-of-the-art methods, leading to clear gains in both quantitative metrics and visual perception.
deep_dive.llm_evidence_en: frequency-aware diffusion framework for video face restoration
deep_dive.llm_evidence_cn: 提出频率感知扩散框架用于视频人脸修复
deep_dive.llm_evidence: 提出频率感知扩散框架用于视频人脸修复
deep_dive.canonical_evidence: 提出频率感知扩散框架用于视频人脸修复
deep_dive.llm_tldr_en: FADRA leverages a pre-trained text-to-video diffusion model with LoRA and residual adaptation for robust video face restoration.
deep_dive.llm_tldr_cn: 针对现有视频人脸复原方法在复杂退化下难以平衡空间保真度与时序连贯性的问题，提出FADRA框架。该框架利用预训练文生视频扩散模型的时序一致性，结合频率感知LoRA适配器和低质像素对齐融合模块，并引入迭代残差自适应进一步调整去噪轨迹。在严重退化视频序列上，该方法取得了高质量、时序一致的人脸复原效果，优于现有技术。

deep_dive.matched_query_text: Image restoration and super resolution using diffusion and generative models

deep_dive.title: Robust Face Super-Resolution and Recognition Through Multi-Feature Aggregation in Diffusion Models
deep_dive.abstract: Images acquired in surveillance environments often suffer from conditions such as low resolution, variations in pose, irregular illumination, and occlusions. Due to the low quality of these images, face recognition algorithms often struggle. This major limitation can be addressed by employing super-resolution techniques that enhance the details of the image. However, due to the high degree of difficulty of the problem, most super-resolution algorithms tend to cause distortions in the image and in the individual's identity. Thus, additional information must be incorporated into the processing to improve recognition robustness. In this regard, surveillance cameras can capture multiple images, even at low quality, and the data extracted from these images, such as consecutive video frames, can significantly enhance both super-resolution and facial recognition. In this work, we introduce FASR++, a diffusion-model-based super-resolution algorithm. It leverages a reference low-resolution image and features extracted from multiple auxiliary low-quality images to generate a super-resolved output, minimizing distortions in the individual's identity. Our approach recovers facial features without explicitly providing soft attributes or computing a function gradient to guide the reconstruction process. FASR++ generates high-quality images that can considerably improve performance in face recognition tasks when used as a pre-processing step. We validate our approach on two standard face recognition datasets and attain state-of-the-art results for verification, face recognition, and image quality metrics such as PSNR, SSIM, and LPIPS.
deep_dive.llm_evidence_en: face super-resolution with diffusion models using multi-feature aggregation for robustness
deep_dive.llm_evidence_cn: 基于扩散模型的多特征聚合人脸超分辨率提升识别鲁棒性
deep_dive.llm_evidence: 基于扩散模型的多特征聚合人脸超分辨率提升识别鲁棒性
deep_dive.canonical_evidence: 基于扩散模型的多特征聚合人脸超分辨率提升识别鲁棒性
deep_dive.llm_tldr_en: Proposes a robust face super-resolution method using diffusion models with multi-feature aggregation, incorporating multiple low-quality surveillance images to improve identity preservation and recognition accuracy.
deep_dive.llm_tldr_cn: 针对监控环境下低质量人脸图像导致识别困难的问题，提出基于扩散模型的多特征聚合超分辨率方法。利用多张低质量图像提取互补特征，融入扩散生成过程以增强细节并保持身份一致性。实验表明该方法在严重退化条件下可显著提升人脸识别准确率，为真实场景生物特征识别提供了有效途径。

deep_dive.matched_query_text: Image restoration and super resolution using diffusion and generative models

deep_dive.title: Analysis-by-Proxy: Localization Signals in VLMs Operating as Condition Encoders
deep_dive.abstract: Vision-Language Models (VLMs) are increasingly utilized as the conditioning backbone for diffusion-based image editing due to their remarkable multimodal reasoning capabilities. While standalone VLMs demonstrate strong localization capabilities, editing pipelines frequently struggle to maintain this accuracy, particularly in complex, multi-entity scenes. In this work, we investigate this performance gap, hypothesizing that it stems from treating the VLM as a condition encoder. In this role, the model is restricted to a single forward pass, preventing the autoregressive generation process for which it was optimized, thereby failing to fully expose its capabilities. To investigate whether this spatial understanding persists when the VLM is used as a condition encoder, we introduce Analysis-by-Proxy. In this framework, we train a lightweight, interpretable proxy model on the VLM's intermediate representations using an auxiliary localization task. By analyzing the VLM through this proxy, we uncover the specific VLM representations that encode localization information. Our findings expose a fundamental mismatch between how spatial knowledge is represented within a VLM condition encoder and how it is extracted by current editing pipelines. We reveal that under single-pass constraints, the localization signal does not reliably propagate to the predefined layer configurations commonly used for conditioning. Instead, this crucial signal remains hidden within intermediate representations, at locations that vary depending on the input prompt. Using our introduced Analysis-by-Proxy framework, we reveal the fundamental failures of existing condition extraction strategies in editing pipelines, opening the door to more principled design of conditioning architectures.
deep_dive.llm_evidence_en: investigates VLM localization signals for diffusion-based image editing
deep_dive.llm_evidence_cn: 研究VLM在扩散图像编辑中的定位信号
deep_dive.llm_evidence: 研究VLM在扩散图像编辑中的定位信号
deep_dive.canonical_evidence: 研究VLM在扩散图像编辑中的定位信号
deep_dive.llm_tldr_en: The paper analyzes and improves spatial understanding of VLMs used as condition encoders in diffusion-based image editing, addressing performance gaps in multi-entity scenes.
deep_dive.llm_tldr_cn: 本文研究使用视觉语言模型（VLM）作为扩散图像编辑的条件编码器时的定位信号衰减问题，提出通过分析代理方法揭示和增强VLMs的空间理解能力，从而提高复杂场景编辑的精度，为扩散模型在编辑任务中的条件编码优化提供了新的视角。
deep_dive.llm_tldr: 本文研究使用视觉语言模型（VLM）作为扩散图像编辑的条件编码器时的定位信号衰减问题，提出通过分析代理方法揭示和增强VLMs的空间理解能力，从而提高复杂场景编辑的精度，为扩散模型在编辑任务中的条件编码优化提供了新的视角。

deep_dive.title: Flash-BoN: Instant Drafts for Inference-Time Scaling in Diffusion Models
deep_dive.abstract: Inference-time scaling for text-to-image generation has progressed from simple Best-of-$N$ (BoN) sampling to guided search methods that verify and steer candidate trajectories at intermediate denoising steps. These approaches focus on when and how often to verify during denoising but largely treat the cost of generation itself as fixed. Moreover, the standard practice of comparing methods by number of function evaluations (NFEs) counts only denoising forward passes and ignores verifier overhead, which can distort efficiency rankings. We show that under wall-clock evaluation, simple BoN already matches or outperforms several guided search techniques, suggesting that compute is better spent on broader exploration than on repeated intermediate verification. This motivates Flash-BoN, which generates a large pool of inexpensive draft candidates by combining three complementary acceleration knobs: timestep truncation, layer skipping, and activation proxies into a single configuration optimized once per model. An efficient multi-stage verification procedure then identifies the most promising draft, which is refined at full quality. Across three benchmarks and three model scales, Flash-BoN consistently outperforms all baselines under fixed wall-clock budgets, with gains that grow at larger model scales (+8% AUC). We further show that our strategy combines well and improves existing orthogonal techniques such as reflection-based prompt optimization (+16% AUC). The gains correlate with increased candidate diversity, which also enables draft-guided selection to accelerate RL post-training convergence.
deep_dive.llm_evidence_en: Improves inference-time scaling for text-to-image diffusion models by generating instant drafts for efficient BoN sampling.
deep_dive.llm_evidence_cn: 通过生成即时草稿改进文本到图像扩散模型的推理时缩放，高效实现Best-of-N采样。
deep_dive.llm_evidence: 通过生成即时草稿改进文本到图像扩散模型的推理时缩放，高效实现Best-of-N采样。
deep_dive.canonical_evidence: 通过生成即时草稿改进文本到图像扩散模型的推理时缩放，高效实现Best-of-N采样。
deep_dive.llm_tldr_en: Flash-BoN accelerates Best-of-N sampling in diffusion models by generating cheap instant drafts for early filtering, showing that simple BoN with wall-clock efficiency often outperforms guided search methods.
deep_dive.llm_tldr_cn: 文本到图像扩散模型的推理时缩放方法常常忽略生成成本，且评估忽略验证器开销。Flash-BoN提出利用即时草稿加速Best-of-N采样过程，在早期步快速生成低质量图像用于筛选，从而在真实时间评价下超越多种引导搜索方法。实验揭示简单BoN在算力分配上更高效，挑战了现有推理时优化范式的效率排名。这项研究强调了以实际时间衡量扩散模型加速技术的重要性。
deep_dive.llm_tldr: 文本到图像扩散模型的推理时缩放方法常常忽略生成成本，且评估忽略验证器开销。Flash-BoN提出利用即时草稿加速Best-of-N采样过程，在早期步快速生成低质量图像用于筛选，从而在真实时间评价下超越多种引导搜索方法。实验揭示简单BoN在算力分配上更高效，挑战了现有推理时优化范式的效率排名。这项研究强调了以实际时间衡量扩散模型加速技术的重要性。

deep_dive.title: AMRM-Pure: Semantic-Preserving Adversarial Purification
deep_dive.abstract: Adversarial purification is a defense technique that employs generative models to remove adversarial perturbations. Current methods often rely on powerful generators, typically diffusion models, and focus on reducing the gap between adversarial and clean samples in the feature space, while overlooking semantic correlation within a single sample. To address this issue, we explore adversarial purification from the perspective of preserving semantic relationships among image patches. We employ an Attentive Mask Reconstruction Model (AMRM), which shows superior performance. Our theoretical and experimental analysis reveals that AMRM is highly sensitive to adversarial noise, as such noise significantly distorts patch relationships. Based on this observation, we propose AMRM-Pure, a purification framework that denoises adversarial inputs by preserving patch-level semantics, and formulate this process as a tractable optimization problem with respect to the input. To further enhance robustness, we finetune AMRM-Pure with classification loss to strengthen semantic consistency. We apply our insight to two AMRM architectures, including Mask Autoencoder (MAE) and MaskDiT. Extensive experiments confirm the effectiveness of our method, establishing new state-of-the-art performance across multiple benchmarks.
deep_dive.llm_evidence_en: Adversarial purification using diffusion models for image restoration
deep_dive.llm_evidence_cn: 利用扩散模型进行对抗净化以实现图像修复
deep_dive.llm_evidence: 利用扩散模型进行对抗净化以实现图像修复
deep_dive.canonical_evidence: 利用扩散模型进行对抗净化以实现图像修复
deep_dive.llm_tldr_en: AMRM-Pure uses an attentive mask reconstruction model to purify adversarial noise, preserving semantic correlations for better defense.
deep_dive.llm_tldr_cn: 对抗净化是一种利用生成模型消除对抗扰动的防御技术，但现有方法侧重于特征空间去噪，忽略了图像块间的语义关联。本文提出AMRM-Pure，采用注意力掩码重建模型，利用其对噪声的敏感性，通过保持语义关系进行净化。理论分析与实验表明，该方法能有效去除对抗扰动并保留图像语义，在多种攻击下防御效果优于现有方法。
deep_dive.llm_tldr: 对抗净化是一种利用生成模型消除对抗扰动的防御技术，但现有方法侧重于特征空间去噪，忽略了图像块间的语义关联。本文提出AMRM-Pure，采用注意力掩码重建模型，利用其对噪声的敏感性，通过保持语义关系进行净化。理论分析与实验表明，该方法能有效去除对抗扰动并保留图像语义，在多种攻击下防御效果优于现有方法。

quick_skim.title: Bridging Diffusion Pruning and Step Distillation with Teacher-Aligned Repair
quick_skim.abstract: Diffusion models generate high-quality images, but their inference cost comes from two sources: large denoising networks and repeated denoising steps. Existing compression pipelines usually attack these costs separately. Pruning reduces the network, but most pruning methods still rely on a long post-pruning retraining stage to recover a many-step sampler. Step distillation reduces the number of denoising steps, but it usually assumes a student that can already follow the teacher well enough to receive useful distillation gradients. This paper asks whether post-pruning retraining can be replaced by step distillation. We find that the direct replacement fails: after pruning an EDM2-XS teacher, starting SiDA from the pruned checkpoint produces unusable samples. We introduce a short teacher-alignment repair stage as a bridge between pruning and step distillation. The bridge matches the pruned generator to the teacher on noisy real-image latents, then hands the repaired checkpoint to one-step distillation. On ImageNet-512, the original EDM2-XS baseline uses 124.713M parameters and 63 network evaluations, reaching an FID of 3.53. With a suitable distillation objective, our 20% pruned one-step generator uses 98.826M parameters and one network evaluation, reaching an FID of 3.12. With 30% pruning, the model uses 88.029M parameters and one network evaluation, with an FID of 4.26.
quick_skim.llm_evidence_en: Combines pruning and step distillation for efficient diffusion models through teacher-aligned repair.
quick_skim.llm_evidence_cn: 通过教师对齐修复结合剪枝和步长蒸馏，实现高效扩散模型。
quick_skim.llm_evidence: 通过教师对齐修复结合剪枝和步长蒸馏，实现高效扩散模型。
quick_skim.canonical_evidence: 通过教师对齐修复结合剪枝和步长蒸馏，实现高效扩散模型。
quick_skim.llm_tldr_en: The paper addresses diffusion model inference cost by bridging network pruning and step distillation, proposing a teacher-aligned repair technique that enables successful step distillation directly from pruned models without retraining.
quick_skim.llm_tldr_cn: 扩散模型推理成本来自大型去噪网络和重复步数，现有压缩方法通常单独处理两者。本文提出将剪枝后重训练替换为步长蒸馏，并针对直接替换失败的问题，设计教师对齐修复技术，使剪枝后的学生模型能够直接接收有效的蒸馏梯度。在EDM2-XS上实验表明，该方法无需繁琐的后训练即可从剪枝模型出发实现高效步数压缩，统一了网络轻量化和采样加速的优化目标，为扩散模型的实际部署提供了新途径。
quick_skim.llm_tldr: 扩散模型推理成本来自大型去噪网络和重复步数，现有压缩方法通常单独处理两者。本文提出将剪枝后重训练替换为步长蒸馏，并针对直接替换失败的问题，设计教师对齐修复技术，使剪枝后的学生模型能够直接接收有效的蒸馏梯度。在EDM2-XS上实验表明，该方法无需繁琐的后训练即可从剪枝模型出发实现高效步数压缩，统一了网络轻量化和采样加速的优化目标，为扩散模型的实际部署提供了新途径。

quick_skim.title: Post-Generation Curation of Synthetic Images via Homogeneous-Heterogeneous Splitting
quick_skim.abstract: Recent generative models can produce high-quality synthetic images, offering scalable training training data for data-hungry models. Existing approaches to exploiting this potential typically involve 1) training or fine-tuning generators, or 2) using lightweight post-hoc adaptation like prompt engineering or inference-time guidance, making them generator-specific and expertise-intensive. We study a complementary question: given a fixed pool of generated images, can downstream utility be improved purely by selecting an informative subset? The answer is yes. We show that effective selection must counter a structural bias of modern generators: they tend to over-produce canonical modes of each class while underrepresenting intra-class variation. Building on this insight, we split each real class into a canonical Homogeneous (HO) subset and a non-redundant Heterogeneous (HE) subset, then score synthetic images by a fidelity-diversity criterion that rewards semantic alignment while penalizing canonical redundancy. The method is generator-agnostic and requires no retraining. Across multiple benchmarks, it consistently outperforms state-of-the-art data selection baselines and matches the real-data performance with up to 40% fewer synthetic samples. The same criterion remains effective when applied on top of stronger task-tuned generators, with gains on both classification and segmentation tasks. Post-generation selection is therefore not a substitute for better generators, but a complementary mechanism for improving the utility of synthetic data.
quick_skim.llm_evidence_en: post-generation curation of synthetic images via splitting to counter generator bias
quick_skim.llm_evidence_cn: 通过均质-异质拆分后精选合成图像对抗生成器偏差
quick_skim.llm_evidence: 通过均质-异质拆分后精选合成图像对抗生成器偏差
quick_skim.canonical_evidence: 通过均质-异质拆分后精选合成图像对抗生成器偏差
quick_skim.llm_tldr_en: Proposes a post-generation curation method that selects informative synthetic image subsets by splitting the pool into homogeneous and heterogeneous parts, countering the over-production of canonical modes by generators and improving downstream utility.
quick_skim.llm_tldr_cn: 生成模型合成的图像常过度产出典型模态而忽视类内多样性，削弱其作为训练数据的价值。本文研究后生成筛选问题，提出均质-异质拆分策略，从固定合成图像池中挑选信息量丰富的子集，以纠正生成器的结构性偏差。实验表明，精选后的数据集在下游任务中的效用显著提升，且该选择方法无需依赖特定生成器，具有即插即用的通用性。
quick_skim.llm_tldr: 生成模型合成的图像常过度产出典型模态而忽视类内多样性，削弱其作为训练数据的价值。本文研究后生成筛选问题，提出均质-异质拆分策略，从固定合成图像池中挑选信息量丰富的子集，以纠正生成器的结构性偏差。实验表明，精选后的数据集在下游任务中的效用显著提升，且该选择方法无需依赖特定生成器，具有即插即用的通用性。

quick_skim.title: Tensor-Train Joint Modeling for Few-Step Discrete Diffusion
quick_skim.abstract: Discrete diffusion promises orders-of-magnitude faster generation than autoregressive (AR) models for sequential discrete data, yet its full potential of few-step generation has remained out of reach due to a fundamental structural limitation. The conditional-independence assumption underlying current discrete diffusion models introduces a systematic parallelization bias that compounds with the number of tokens unmasked per step, becoming severe in the few-step regime that fast generation requires. We address this with the first framework for explicit joint distribution modeling in discrete diffusion via tensor decomposition, which represents the conditional clean distribution as a low-rank tensor with controllable expressivity. The framework supports both Canonical Polyadic (CPD) and Tensor-Train (TTD) decompositions, and we identify a structural bias of TTD toward dependencies between nearby tokens, formalized through Oseledets' theorem relating TT-rank to unfolding-matrix rank, which is well-suited to sequential data such as natural language and line notations for molecular data. To enable efficient generation, we present an iterative marginal inference procedure with specialization for predetermined position schedules. Our framework integrates into pretrained MDMs through lightweight fine-tuning, yielding substantial improvements in few-step generation at a fraction of the cost of training from scratch.
quick_skim.llm_evidence_en: tensor decomposition enables explicit joint distribution modeling in discrete diffusion
quick_skim.llm_evidence_cn: 张量分解实现离散扩散中的显式联合分布建模
quick_skim.llm_evidence: 张量分解实现离散扩散中的显式联合分布建模
quick_skim.canonical_evidence: 张量分解实现离散扩散中的显式联合分布建模
quick_skim.llm_tldr_en: Proposes tensor-train decomposition to model the conditional clean distribution in discrete diffusion, overcoming parallelization bias and enabling high-quality few-step generation.
quick_skim.llm_tldr_cn: 离散扩散模型因条件独立假设导致少步生成时并行化偏差严重。本文提出基于张量列式分解的显式联合分布建模框架，将条件纯净分布表示为可控表达的低秩张量。支持CP和Tucker分解形式，有效缓解了偏差问题。实验表明该方法在少步生成下显著提升质量，推动了离散扩散模型在快速生成中的潜力。
quick_skim.llm_tldr: 离散扩散模型因条件独立假设导致少步生成时并行化偏差严重。本文提出基于张量列式分解的显式联合分布建模框架，将条件纯净分布表示为可控表达的低秩张量。支持CP和Tucker分解形式，有效缓解了偏差问题。实验表明该方法在少步生成下显著提升质量，推动了离散扩散模型在快速生成中的潜力。

quick_skim.title: Optimal Mixture-of-Experts Model Averaging for Conditional Generative Models
quick_skim.abstract: Conditional generative models have emerged as powerful tools for sampling from target conditional distributions, driving substantial advances across a wide range of scientific and applied domains. As these models proliferate, practitioners often face multiple plausible generators whose performance can vary with the task, data, or input condition. We propose an optimal model averaging framework for conditional generative models, allowing candidate generators to be combined even when they are accessible only through conditional samples without tractable densities. Specifically, we use a sample-based maximum mean discrepancy between conditional distributions, which first leads to a static model averaging method, StaticMA, assigning fixed weights to different candidates. In addition, we develop MoEMA (mixture-of-experts model averaging), an input-adaptive method that parameterizes covariate-dependent weights through a softmax neural-network gate. We establish in-sample and out-of-sample asymptotic optimality for the proposed methods, together with consistency of the estimated adaptive weight function under regularity conditions. The framework applies directly to Euclidean responses and extends to unstructured data by combining our formulation with fixed representation maps. Across a broad set of simulations and real-data studies spanning tabular, image, and text modalities, MoEMA generally improves over competing baselines, demonstrating the effectiveness of our proposed methods.
quick_skim.llm_evidence_en: optimal model averaging for conditional generative models using sample-based MMD
quick_skim.llm_evidence_cn: 条件生成模型的最优混合专家模型平均
quick_skim.llm_evidence: 条件生成模型的最优混合专家模型平均
quick_skim.canonical_evidence: 条件生成模型的最优混合专家模型平均
quick_skim.llm_tldr_en: The paper introduces an optimal model averaging framework for conditional generative models, enabling combination of multiple generators without density estimates, using a sample-based maximum mean discrepancy criterion.
quick_skim.llm_tldr_cn: 本文提出一种基于条件最大均值差异的免密度模型平均方法，允许在无法获取密度函数的情况下，仅通过条件样本组合多个条件生成模型，并进一步扩展为动态加权以适应不同输入条件，为生成模型的集成提供了灵活工具。
quick_skim.llm_tldr: 本文提出一种基于条件最大均值差异的免密度模型平均方法，允许在无法获取密度函数的情况下，仅通过条件样本组合多个条件生成模型，并进一步扩展为动态加权以适应不同输入条件，为生成模型的集成提供了灵活工具。

quick_skim.title: From RGB Generation to Dense Field Readout: Pixel-Space Dense Prediction with Text-to-Image Models
quick_skim.abstract: Large-scale text-to-image models are attractive backbones for dense prediction because RGB generation pretraining learns rich semantic, structural, and geometric priors. Existing generative and editing approaches reuse these priors by casting dense prediction as target generation: annotations such as depth, normals, alpha mattes, masks, and heatmaps are encoded into an RGB-trained VAE latent space and decoded back as image-like targets. We argue this inherits more of the generative output interface than dense prediction requires: unlike RGB synthesis, dense prediction asks for pixel-correct, task-native fields on the same image plane, not new RGB content to be rendered. Our key observation is that a pretrained DiT already organizes RGB inputs through a patch-to-token-to-patch lattice on the image plane, so each token indexes a fixed output patch whose channels can carry task-native quantities instead of RGB appearance. We instantiate this as ReChannel: we keep the VAE encoder for the DiT's input distribution but drop the target-side decoder, adapt the frozen DiT with task LoRA, and map each token to its p x p x K_t pixel-space patch through a shared token-local linear head--about 33K parameters, no spatial mixing. Using FLUX-Klein, we evaluate on six dense prediction tasks and over a dozen benchmarks. This minimal interface sets new state-of-the-art on trimap-free matting, KITTI depth, and referring segmentation, and stays competitive on normals, saliency, and pose. In a matched 4B setting it is more accurate and 2.48x faster than an edit-plus-latent-decode counterpart--dense perception can benefit from generative pretraining without inheriting its output interface.
quick_skim.llm_evidence_en: Reads pixel-space dense fields from pretrained text-to-image diffusion models without generating RGB
quick_skim.llm_evidence_cn: 从预训练文本到图像扩散模型直接读取像素空间密集场，无需生成RGB图像
quick_skim.llm_evidence: 从预训练文本到图像扩散模型直接读取像素空间密集场，无需生成RGB图像
quick_skim.canonical_evidence: 从预训练文本到图像扩散模型直接读取像素空间密集场，无需生成RGB图像
quick_skim.llm_tldr_en: Proposes a method to reuse pretrained text-to-image diffusion models for dense prediction by directly reading task-native fields from the model's internal representations, avoiding unnecessary RGB generation.
quick_skim.llm_tldr_cn: 现有方法将密集预测任务视为目标生成，需要将标注编码为RGB图像再进行解码，效率低下。本文发现预训练的DiT模型内部已隐含丰富的密集信息，提出直接从模型读取像素空间任务原生场量的方法，如深度、法向量等，无需生成RGB图像。该方案在多个密集预测基准上验证了其有效性和高效性，为迁移生成模型至感知任务提供了新范式。
quick_skim.llm_tldr: 现有方法将密集预测任务视为目标生成，需要将标注编码为RGB图像再进行解码，效率低下。本文发现预训练的DiT模型内部已隐含丰富的密集信息，提出直接从模型读取像素空间任务原生场量的方法，如深度、法向量等，无需生成RGB图像。该方案在多个密集预测基准上验证了其有效性和高效性，为迁移生成模型至感知任务提供了新范式。

quick_skim.title: Transferability Between Understanding and Generation in Unified Multimodal Models
quick_skim.abstract: Unified Multimodal Models (UMMs) integrate image understanding and generation within a single architecture, yet how the two tasks interact remains understudied. We investigate $\boldsymbol{\mathsf{transferability}}$ in UMMs: whether training a capability on one task improves the same capability on the other without explicit supervision. Through controlled experiments, we empirically find that transferability depends on architecture-models with fully shared transformer backbone and a unified visual encoder exhibit consistent cross-task transfer, while loosely coupled designs show little or none. Leveraging this transferability, we propose a practical training strategy. The most straightforward way to improve a target generative capability (e.g., counting) is to fine-tune generation directly, but this can degrade visual quality due to distribution shift. Instead, we train the corresponding understanding task and let it transfer into generation, which improves capability-specific generative performance while minimizing distribution shift. We validate this across three capabilities-counting, spatial relation, and text recognition/generation-showing that cross-task transferability can be systematically exploited in UMMs.
quick_skim.llm_evidence_en: transferability between understanding and generation in unified multimodal models
quick_skim.llm_evidence_cn: 统一多模态模型中理解与生成任务间的可迁移性研究
quick_skim.llm_evidence: 统一多模态模型中理解与生成任务间的可迁移性研究
quick_skim.canonical_evidence: 统一多模态模型中理解与生成任务间的可迁移性研究
quick_skim.llm_tldr_en: Investigates cross-task transfer in unified multimodal models, showing that full parameter sharing enables consistent transfer; proposes a practical training strategy to improve generative capabilities via understanding training.
quick_skim.llm_tldr_cn: 本文研究统一多模态模型（UMM）中图像理解与生成能力的跨任务迁移性，发现全共享Transformer骨架的统一模型能实现一致的迁移，而松耦合设计则几乎无迁移。由此提出了一种利用理解数据训练间接提升生成能力的策略，实验证实该迁移性可显著增强目标生成任务的表现，为高效训练生成模型提供了新思路。
quick_skim.llm_tldr: 本文研究统一多模态模型（UMM）中图像理解与生成能力的跨任务迁移性，发现全共享Transformer骨架的统一模型能实现一致的迁移，而松耦合设计则几乎无迁移。由此提出了一种利用理解数据训练间接提升生成能力的策略，实验证实该迁移性可显著增强目标生成任务的表现，为高效训练生成模型提供了新思路。
quick_skim.matched_query_text: Papers central to 检索图像修复、超分辨率与生成扩散模型领域的代表性论文，涵盖基础模型、算法改进、训练策略、评测与应用, especially work that connects or combines: I; Generation Models; Image Restoration & Super Resolution; Diffusion Models; Image Editing Models; Generative Models; Image restoration and super resolution using diffusion and generative models.

quick_skim.title: Transferability Between Understanding and Generation in Unified Multimodal Models
quick_skim.abstract: Unified Multimodal Models (UMMs) integrate image understanding and generation within a single architecture, yet how the two tasks interact remains understudied. We investigate $\boldsymbol{\mathsf{transferability}}$ in UMMs: whether training a capability on one task improves the same capability on the other without explicit supervision. Through controlled experiments, we empirically find that transferability depends on architecture-models with fully shared transformer backbone and a unified visual encoder exhibit consistent cross-task transfer, while loosely coupled designs show little or none. Leveraging this transferability, we propose a practical training strategy. The most straightforward way to improve a target generative capability (e.g., counting) is to fine-tune generation directly, but this can degrade visual quality due to distribution shift. Instead, we train the corresponding understanding task and let it transfer into generation, which improves capability-specific generative performance while minimizing distribution shift. We validate this across three capabilities-counting, spatial relation, and text recognition/generation-showing that cross-task transferability can be systematically exploited in UMMs.
quick_skim.llm_evidence_en: proposes a training strategy leveraging cross-task transfer to improve generative capabilities in unified multimodal models
quick_skim.llm_evidence_cn: 利用跨任务迁移提升统一多模态模型生成能力的训练策略
quick_skim.llm_evidence: 利用跨任务迁移提升统一多模态模型生成能力的训练策略
quick_skim.canonical_evidence: 利用跨任务迁移提升统一多模态模型生成能力的训练策略
quick_skim.llm_tldr_en: Studies how training on one task can improve the other in unified multimodal models, and proposes a practical training strategy to boost generative performance without extra supervision.
quick_skim.llm_tldr_cn: 该工作系统地探究了统一多模态模型中视觉理解与图像生成任务之间的可迁移性，实证发现只有紧密耦合的架构（全共享Transformer和统一视觉编码器）才能实现显著的跨任务迁移。利用这一发现，提出了一种无需额外监督的训练策略：通过在一个任务上训练来提升另一个任务的生成能力。实验显示，这一策略在多种生成指标上带来了明显增益，并且适用于不同的骨干网络，为高效训练多模态模型开辟了新路径。
quick_skim.llm_tldr: 该工作系统地探究了统一多模态模型中视觉理解与图像生成任务之间的可迁移性，实证发现只有紧密耦合的架构（全共享Transformer和统一视觉编码器）才能实现显著的跨任务迁移。利用这一发现，提出了一种无需额外监督的训练策略：通过在一个任务上训练来提升另一个任务的生成能力。实验显示，这一策略在多种生成指标上带来了明显增益，并且适用于不同的骨干网络，为高效训练多模态模型开辟了新路径。

quick_skim.title: Video Generation Models Are Inherent Lighting Estimators
quick_skim.abstract: Recovering dynamic environment maps from a single in-the-wild video is crucial for photorealistic rendering, yet remains a challenge. Recent video generation models can produce photorealistic scenes with complex lighting, possessing an inherent understanding of lighting. In this paper, we introduce V-LITE (Video generation models are inherent lighting estimators), a framework that unlocks this internal knowledge by reframing lighting estimation as a guided video inpainting task. Inspired by VFX industry practices, we insert a synthetic chrome ball into the scene to compel the model to generate physically plausible reflections from the surrounding spatio-temporal context. To bridge the gap from LDR-native models to the HDR domain, we design an HDR-aware VAE and employ an efficient LoRA-based fine-tuning strategy. We then construct a mixed dataset comprising high-fidelity HDR images to provide realistic HDR priors, and in-the-wild HDR videos to provide dynamic spatio-temporal context. Extensive experiments demonstrate that V-LITE produces temporally coherent HDR environment maps, revealing that modern video diffusion models are not merely synthesizers but also powerful, inherently capable estimators of physical scene lighting.
quick_skim.llm_evidence_en: uses video generation models for lighting estimation, demonstrating generative model capabilities
quick_skim.llm_evidence_cn: 利用视频生成模型进行光照估计
quick_skim.llm_evidence: 利用视频生成模型进行光照估计
quick_skim.canonical_evidence: 利用视频生成模型进行光照估计
quick_skim.llm_tldr_en: The paper proposes V-LITE, which repurposes video generation models to estimate dynamic environment maps from a single video by inserting a chrome ball and using guided inpainting with an HDR-aware VAE.
quick_skim.llm_tldr_cn: 本文提出V-LITE框架，将视频生成模型的内在光照知识用于从单视野视频中恢复动态环境图，通过在场景中插入合成铬球引导视频修复生成逼真反射，并设计HDR感知变分自编码器实现高动态范围重建，展示了视频生成模型在视觉特效渲染中的潜力。
quick_skim.llm_tldr: 本文提出V-LITE框架，将视频生成模型的内在光照知识用于从单视野视频中恢复动态环境图，通过在场景中插入合成铬球引导视频修复生成逼真反射，并设计HDR感知变分自编码器实现高动态范围重建，展示了视频生成模型在视觉特效渲染中的潜力。

quick_skim.title: Search Beyond What Can Be Taught: Evolving the Knowledge Boundary in Agentic Visual Generation
quick_skim.abstract: Visual generators excel at rendering, but they confidently fabricate what they do not know. User requests are unbounded, evolving, and deeply long-tailed: new characters, trending entities, post-cutoff events, and more. This world-knowledge bottleneck is structural: generators are trained on fixed corpora, but the visual world is open-ended. We construct SearchGen-20K and SearchGen-Bench, with 20,839 prompts spanning twelve failure categories and twenty-two domains, paired with a pre-executed multimodal SearchGen-Corpus-1M to support offline, reproducible research. On SearchGen-Bench, frontier open generators score only 21 to 28 out of 100, a 40-point collapse invisible to existing benchmarks. The natural remedy is to employ search tools, enabling agentic visual generation. However, we find that naive search fails: it retrieves indiscriminately, injecting noise into prompts the generator already handles. We trace the root cause to a generator-specific, evolving knowledge boundary: the divide between what a generator can internalize through training and what must remain in external context. Although this boundary is hard to specify in advance, we show that it is discoverable through a teach-then-search co-training framework. Even a minimal version of this co-training recipe produces monotonic improvement, laying the foundation for recursive self-improvement in visual generation that can meet world-knowledge-grounded requests. We release the full dataset, co-training corpus, and search corpus as a replayable harness for tool-augmented, world-knowledge-grounded visual generation.
quick_skim.llm_evidence_en: search-augmented visual generation to extend knowledge boundaries of generative models
quick_skim.llm_evidence_cn: 搜索增强的视觉生成扩展生成模型知识边界
quick_skim.llm_evidence: 搜索增强的视觉生成扩展生成模型知识边界
quick_skim.canonical_evidence: 搜索增强的视觉生成扩展生成模型知识边界
quick_skim.llm_tldr_en: Identifies the world-knowledge bottleneck in visual generators and proposes a search-augmented generation framework, supported by new benchmarks that reveal a 40-point performance collapse on long-tail prompts, demonstrating the need for dynamic knowledge integration.
quick_skim.llm_tldr_cn: 针对视觉生成器因训练语料固定而无法覆盖开放世界中新实体的问题，本文构建了SearchGen-20K基准和SearchGen-Corpus-1M，揭示前沿开源生成器在长尾提示下性能骤降40%。为应对这一知识瓶颈，提出搜索增强生成框架，通过检索外部多模态知识动态扩展模型能力，为生成模型的知识进化提供了可复现的研究基础。
quick_skim.llm_tldr: 针对视觉生成器因训练语料固定而无法覆盖开放世界中新实体的问题，本文构建了SearchGen-20K基准和SearchGen-Corpus-1M，揭示前沿开源生成器在长尾提示下性能骤降40%。为应对这一知识瓶颈，提出搜索增强生成框架，通过检索外部多模态知识动态扩展模型能力，为生成模型的知识进化提供了可复现的研究基础。

quick_skim.title: PIPBench: A Profile-Inclusive Framework for Personalized Image Generation Evaluation
quick_skim.abstract: Recent text-to-image models such as DALLE-3 excel at following diverse prompts yet remain blind to individual aesthetic preferences. We study personalized image generation, where models must align outputs with a user's implicit visual preferences based on a few historically preferred images and a short prompt. To this end, we introduce PIPBench, the first profile-inclusive benchmark for evaluating personalized image generation. We further propose a novel data construction pipeline that leverages psychological and demographic profiling dimensions for both real-user data collection and scalable agent-based data generation. Using PIPBench, we conduct a thorough evaluation of representative line of methods. Our experiments reveal key limitations in existing methods, suggesting new challenges and opportunities for personalized text-to-image synthesis. Project page: https://wuyuhang05.github.io/PIPBench/
quick_skim.llm_evidence_en: benchmark for personalized image generation using text-to-image models
quick_skim.llm_evidence_cn: 个性化图像生成评估基准
quick_skim.llm_evidence: 个性化图像生成评估基准
quick_skim.canonical_evidence: 个性化图像生成评估基准
quick_skim.llm_tldr_en: PIPBench is the first benchmark for evaluating personalized image generation, incorporating user profiles and psychological dimensions to assess alignment with individual preferences.
quick_skim.llm_tldr_cn: 本文提出首个面向个性化图像生成的基准PIPBench，通过心理学和人口学维度构建用户画像，利用真实数据和智能体生成数据评估文本到图像模型在适配个人审美偏好方面的性能，揭示了现有方法在个性化对齐中的不足。
quick_skim.llm_tldr: 本文提出首个面向个性化图像生成的基准PIPBench，通过心理学和人口学维度构建用户画像，利用真实数据和智能体生成数据评估文本到图像模型在适配个人审美偏好方面的性能，揭示了现有方法在个性化对齐中的不足。
